_comment: Custom seccomp profile for honeypot containers. Base: Docker's default profile, narrowed for a Python TCP/UDP listener workload. NOT validated against a live container runtime in this exercise (no Docker daemon in this build environment) — test with `docker run --security-opt seccomp=honeypot-seccomp.json` and watch for EPERM/ENOSYS errors before relying on this in production; an overly strict profile fails closed (the honeypot won't start) rather than open, but it must still be verified against the actual interpreter + libc syscalls used at runtime.

syscalls.comment: Core process/memory management the Python interpreter needs
Networking — required for the honeypot's actual job (accept connections)

Filesystem ops scoped to the app's own writable /data + /tmp (the kernel doesn't path-scope syscalls, so this only limits WHICH syscalls run, not WHERE — pair with read_only:true + tmpfs + a non-root UID owning only /data so there's nothing sensitive to reach even if these run)
Explicitly-named DENY for syscalls associated with privilege escalation / container breakout primitives — redundant with defaultAction:ERRNO but documents intent
